//2: ErrorBoundary.tsx is excluded as unreachable-by-design: expo-router wraps every route in its own error boundary, so route render crashes never propagate to this component (verified by intercepting a recipe payload that guarantees a React child-type crash — expo-router recovered to home). It only guards provider/layout-level failures, which no browser flow can trigger. Making the branded fallback actually reachable requires exporting route-level ErrorBoundary adapters (tracked as a Backlog story).
//: E2E coverage scope policy: coverage is measured over everything chromium E2E can actually execute. The TARGET is 100%; the thresholds below are DEFINED LOWER BOUNDS (a safety net), not the goal. Native-only branches (Platform.OS !== 'web': Alert.alert, SecureStore) are excluded at the source level via '/* istanbul ignore */' comments with per-site justification — 100% unit-tested instead — rather than accommodated by a lower floor. New code ships at 100%. Floors only ratchet UP as gaps close (household, profile, cook mode, notify() web path, client.ts error paths, ErrorBoundary); never lower them to make CI pass — write the tests or add a justified source-level exclusion.
